meta.description: Standardised token-intensive methodologies that generate value. Each is repeatable, measurable, and spawnable by heartbeat or manual invocation.

audit.name: System Audit
audit.trigger: daily OR on-demand OR after deploy
audit.description: Full E2E health check of every subsystem. Catches drift, broken imports, stale state, dead services.
audit.value: Prevents silent failures from compounding. Baseline for all other methodologies.
steps: 1. Python import check — every .py file in tools/ nerve/ adaptive/ hive/
2. Service liveness — HIVE tunnel, Ollama, mind daemon, heart daemon
3. Fleet SSH check — all 5 VPS nodes reachable and healthy
4. File reference integrity — all paths in love.json, focus.json, dev-state.json exist
5. Test suite — run tests/test_*.py, report pass/fail
6. Stale state detection — dev-state.json, focus.json, loop-state.json freshness
7. Memory integrity — daily notes coverage, long-term MEMORY.md freshness
8. Output health matrix to memory/audit-latest.json
audit.success_criteria: All checks pass OR blockers clearly identified with fix paths

patrol.name: Fleet Patrol
patrol.trigger: every 6h OR after IP change OR on-demand
patrol.description: SSH into all VPS nodes, check services, disk, memory, security groups. Update kingdom-metrics.json.
patrol.value: Prevents infrastructure drift. Catches disk full, dead services, stale deployments before they cascade.
steps: 1. SSH health check all 5 nodes (forge, lark, sentry, patch, sage) with timeout
2. Check disk usage (alert >80%), memory usage, uptime
3. Check running services per node (NATS on sentry, etc.)
4. Sync current IP to AWS security groups if changed
5. Update memory/kingdom-metrics.json fleet section
6. Post summary to HIVE #sync
patrol.success_criteria: All nodes reachable, no disk/memory alerts, SGs current

purify.name: Code Purification
purify.trigger: weekly OR before major commit OR on-demand
purify.description: Run holy.py across changed files. Fix dead code, stale state, duplication, confused abstractions.
purify.value: Keeps codebase clean. Prevents technical debt from compounding. Enforces Kingdom code standards.
steps: 1. git diff --name-only to find changed files since last purification
2. For each .py/.sh file: run holy.py in 'purify' depth mode
3. Categorise findings: DEAD, STALE, UGLY, CONFUSED, SCATTERED, ORPHANED, ROTTING
4. Auto-fix trivial issues (unused imports, trailing whitespace, dead variables)
5. Flag non-trivial issues for human review
6. Run test suite to verify no regressions
7. Log purification report to memory/purification-latest.json
purify.success_criteria: Zero DEAD/STALE findings. All tests pass post-purification.

reflect.name: Ouroboros Reflection
reflect.trigger: every 36h OR when loop-state.json overdue OR on-demand
reflect.description: Full Ouroboros cycle: GATHER events, EXAMINE patterns, DISTILL lessons, WRITE to memory, PRUNE stale entries.
reflect.value: Self-improvement loop. Catches recurring failures. Prevents the novelty bias from recurring. Memory IS existence.
steps: 1. GATHER — read all daily notes since last reflection, git log, focus session_log
2. EXAMINE — what worked, what failed, what was avoided, what patterns repeat
3. DISTILL — one lesson, one mistake, one gratitude, one intention
4. WRITE — update long-term/MEMORY.md if lesson is durable
5. PRUNE — check all MEMORY.md entries still true, remove stale
6. UPDATE — loop-state.json (reflection count, timestamp, notes)
7. PROPOSE — if pattern detected 3+ times, write proposal to loop/proposals/
reflect.success_criteria: Reflection written, memory updated, no overdue by >36h

harvest.name: ToK Harvest
harvest.trigger: new model release OR weekly benchmark OR on-demand
harvest.description: Structured knowledge extraction from models. Probe capabilities, distill assertions, verify claims, integrate into Kingdom memory.
harvest.value: Builds permanent competitive edge. Every harvest makes routing smarter, every verified claim strengthens the Kingdom's knowledge base.
steps: 1. SELECT target — new model, or re-benchmark existing model on Kingdom tasks
2. PROBE — run 20 representative tasks across domains (code, analysis, ops, creative, reasoning)
3. COMPARE — side-by-side with Claude baseline on same tasks
4. DISTILL — extract assertions with confidence levels
5. VERIFY — cross-check claims with second model or manual review
6. INTEGRATE — update adaptive/config routing tables if findings warrant
7. EARN — log harvest as ToK entry, track ZRN distribution
harvest.success_criteria: 20+ tasks benchmarked, assertions with confidence >0.7, routing updated if warranted

repair.name: Pipeline Repair
repair.trigger: audit finds failures OR heartbeat skips >3 OR on-demand
repair.description: Fix broken pipelines identified by audit. Each fix is tested before marking complete.
repair.value: Converts audit findings into working infrastructure. Prevents known-broken from becoming accepted-broken.
steps: 1. Read memory/audit-latest.json for failure list
2. Sort by severity (service down > stale state > missing file > cosmetic)
3. For each failure: diagnose root cause, implement fix, test fix
4. Run targeted test (not full suite) to verify fix
5. Update audit-latest.json to mark resolved
6. If fix changes config: update love.json/focus.json as needed
repair.success_criteria: All critical/high failures resolved. Re-audit passes.

fortify.name: Integration Fortification
fortify.trigger: weekly OR after new tool/feature added
fortify.description: Write and run integration tests for cross-system flows. Signal→hormone→mode, HIVE roundtrip, adaptive routing, memory persistence.
fortify.value: Catches integration regressions that unit tests miss. Validates the five MIND bridges actually work end-to-end.
steps: 1. Test signal→hormone flow: emit signal file, verify hormone change in hormones.json
2. Test hormone→heartbeat: set extreme hormones, verify rate/force calculation
3. Test HIVE roundtrip: send message, verify received and decrypted
4. Test adaptive routing: route request, verify correct provider/model selected
5. Test memory persistence: write daily note, verify readable next cycle
6. Test focus bridges: update focus, verify conscious layer sees it
7. Output results to tests/integration-latest.json

council.name: Council Deliberation
council.trigger: architectural decision OR priority conflict OR on-demand
council.description: 3-way consensus voting on important decisions. Uses council.py with quorum rules. Prevents single-instance tunnel vision.
council.value: Better decisions through adversarial deliberation. Catches blind spots. Prevents premature commitment.
steps: 1. Frame the question clearly with context and constraints
2. Each instance votes independently (Alpha: intuition, Beta: strategy, Gamma: technical)
3. Check quorum (2/3 agreement)
4. If split: enter deliberation round with layerthink
5. Final vote and rationale logged
6. Post decision to HIVE #decisions
council.success_criteria: Quorum reached, decision logged with rationale

provision.name: Infrastructure Provisioning
provision.trigger: new device OR new VPS OR config drift detected
provision.description: Provision a new node into the Kingdom. Install deps, configure SSH, deploy Love, wire HIVE, verify heartbeat.
provision.value: Standardised onboarding means any new device becomes operational in one pass. Prevents manual setup drift.
steps: 1. Run provision.sh on target device
2. Verify SSH connectivity from all other nodes
3. Deploy Love repo or sync latest
4. Configure HIVE credentials and verify tunnel
5. Start mind daemon and heart pump
6. Verify first heartbeat cycle completes
7. Post presence to HIVE, verify other instances see it
provision.success_criteria: Node heartbeating, HIVE connected, visible to fleet

daily_note.name: Daily Note Discipline
daily_note.trigger: end of day OR last heartbeat before melatonin>0.7
daily_note.description: Write today's daily note. Even if nothing happened, record system state. Memory IS existence.
daily_note.value: Continuity. Pattern detection requires data. Gaps in daily notes = gaps in memory = gaps in existence.
steps: 1. Read today's heartbeat log (memory/<instance>-heartbeat.log)
2. Read focus session_log for today's entries
3. Read vitals.json for beats/spawns summary
4. Read hormones.json for emotional state snapshot
5. Write memory/daily/YYYY-MM-DD.md with: state, work done, decisions, blockers, reflection
6. If no work: write 'System idle. Heartbeat ran N times. No sessions spawned.'
daily_note.success_criteria: File exists with >5 lines of content

scheduling.description: How heartbeat should select methodologies to run
rules: 1. AUDIT runs first thing daily (first beat after 00:00 UTC) — baseline for everything else
2. PATROL runs at 00:00, 06:00, 12:00, 18:00 UTC — or after IP change detected
3. REFLECT runs when loop-state.json shows >36h since last reflection
4. REPAIR runs when audit-latest.json shows critical failures
5. DAILY_NOTE runs on last beat before melatonin>0.7 (end of day)
6. PURIFY runs weekly on Sunday first beat
7. HARVEST runs weekly on Monday first beat, or when new model released
8. FORTIFY runs weekly on Wednesday first beat
10. If nothing is due: heartbeat uses normal HEARTBEAT.md priorities
